description: Implement an EventEmitter class with on, off, emit, and once methods
prompt: Write a JavaScript ES module that exports a class `EventEmitter` with the following methods: `on(event, listener)` registers a listener for an event, `off(event, listener)` removes a specific listener, `emit(event, ...args)` calls all listeners for the event with the given arguments, and `once(event, listener)` registers a listener that is automatically removed after it fires once. Export as named export.

tests.name: on and emit work together
tests.code: const ee = new mod.EventEmitter(); let called = false; ee.on('test', () => { called = true; }); ee.emit('test'); assert(called === true, 'Listener should have been called');

tests.name: off removes a listener
tests.code: const ee = new mod.EventEmitter(); let count = 0; const fn = () => { count++; }; ee.on('test', fn); ee.emit('test'); ee.off('test', fn); ee.emit('test'); assert(count === 1, 'Expected 1 call after off but got ' + count);

tests.name: once fires listener only once
tests.code: const ee = new mod.EventEmitter(); let count = 0; ee.once('test', () => { count++; }); ee.emit('test'); ee.emit('test'); ee.emit('test'); assert(count === 1, 'Expected once listener called 1 time but got ' + count);

tests.name: supports multiple listeners on same event
tests.code: const ee = new mod.EventEmitter(); const results = []; ee.on('test', () => results.push('a')); ee.on('test', () => results.push('b')); ee.emit('test'); assert(JSON.stringify(results) === JSON.stringify(['a', 'b']), 'Expected [a,b] but got ' + JSON.stringify(results));

tests.name: emit passes arguments to listeners
tests.code: const ee = new mod.EventEmitter(); let received = null; ee.on('data', (a, b) => { received = [a, b]; }); ee.emit('data', 'hello', 42); assert(JSON.stringify(received) === JSON.stringify(['hello', 42]), 'Expected [hello, 42] but got ' + JSON.stringify(received));
